verdict_reasoning: SEZ6 is a type I transmembrane protein containing a signal peptide, multiple extracellular sushi (CCP) and CUB domains, a transmembrane domain, and a cytoplasmic tail. The HGNC gene-group membership 'Sushi domain containing' corroborates a surface topology. Sushi and CUB domains are canonical extracellular motifs found in complement-related and cell-adhesion protein families; their presence in this protein strongly indicates a classical single-pass surface receptor architecture with substantial extracellular domain exposure.
